Off Calendar Jul-22-2010 Continued To Aug-06-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF CATHAY BANK'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY THE APPOINTMENT OF A RECEIVER SHOULD NOT BE CONFIRMED (Per Ex Parte Order Of 06/28/10). Appearances: Shari D. Marks of Frandzel Robins Bloom & Csato, LC (788.7400) for plaintiff and moving party; Mark J. Romeo of the Law Offices of Mark J. Romeo (395.9315) for Oceanic Bank; Michael Schinner of the Schinner Law Group (369.9050) for defendant and responding party.
The matter was not argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted. Plaintiff has made a sufficient showing demonstrating a receiver is necessary to secure and preserve subject property 2, which defendants abandoned in an incomplete and hazardous condition. Receiver also appointed for properties 1 and 3. Defendants agreed to the appointment of a receiver in the underlying loan documents. Defendants have not demonstrated to the Court that less drastic remedies are available or appropriate. The Court continued the matter to August 6, 2010, for parties to agree upon the form of order. Temporary orders remain in full force and effect. Petitioner to submit a proposed form of order regarding the continuance. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
